summary: The dominant failure is wrong_result from SQLWriter mishandling multi-part questions, deriving boolean/qualitative answers, and global-vs-grouped aggregation semantics. SchemaLinker occasionally selects wrong columns/filters and SQLWriter answers only part of multi-clause questions. A smaller cluster stalls before executing SQL because agents ask clarifying questions instead of committing to a reasonable interpretation.

issues.hypothesis: SQLWriter returns raw columns instead of the derived answer the question requires, and mishandles 'which/how many of the max' semantics. Exemplar 1 asks 'was that post well-finished?' (a yes/no about post closure) but the SQL returns 'SELECT p.Id, p.ClosedDate' — a null ClosedDate is not translated into the expected boolean/label. Exemplar 3 asks for players with 'the highest potential score for crossing' but SQL filters 'crossing = (SELECT MAX(crossing))' — it maxes the crossing attribute rather than potential, and applies MAX globally across all attribute rows instead of per-player, yielding cnt=2.
issues.fix_direction: Add guidance: when a question expects a qualitative/boolean verdict (e.g. 'well-finished', 'closed'), map the underlying column to the label the question implies (NULL ClosedDate -> not closed / well-finished) rather than returning the raw column. When a question superlatives on attribute A ('highest potential score for crossing'), identify the metric noun carefully (potential vs crossing) and confirm whether MAX should be computed over the filtered subset (preferred_foot='left') rather than globally.

issues.hypothesis: Multi-part questions get only partially answered. Exemplar 2 (wrong_tables cluster) asks three things — top spender, average price per single item, and currency — and the query computes SUM(Price)/SUM(Amount); but 'average price per single item' likely means AVG over per-transaction unit prices or Price/Amount per row, and the ranking of 'top spending' should be by total spend, which is ambiguous vs Consumption in the yearmonth table. The single query conflates definitions.
issues.fix_direction: Instruct SQLWriter to enumerate each sub-question in the prompt and produce one column per sub-question, and to prefer AVG(Price/Amount) (per-row unit price) when the phrase is 'average price per single item' rather than SUM(Price)/SUM(Amount). Add a checklist step: verify every interrogative clause in the question maps to an output column before handoff.

issues.hypothesis: SchemaLinker links to the wrong filter column/value encoding. Exemplar 2 (card_games) filters 'c.originalType LIKE 'Artif...' but Artifact-ness is typically in the 'type' or 'types' column, not originalType; and 'black color' should filter cards.colors on 'B' code, not a literal. The linker surfaces columns but doesn't flag the coded-value convention (colors stored as 'B','W','U' etc.).
issues.fix_direction: Add memory note for card_games/coded-enum schemas: colors/colorIdentity use single-letter codes (B=black, W=white,...), and card type filters should use the canonical 'type'/'types' column, verified via sample_rows before committing. More generally, when a WHERE literal is a domain term, SchemaLinker should sample distinct values of the candidate column to confirm the stored encoding and pass the exact literal to SQLWriter.

issues.hypothesis: Runs terminate with no SQL executed because an upstream agent asks the user a clarifying question instead of committing. Exemplar 3 (toxicology) ends with FINAL ANSWER: 'one quick clarification before I hand this to the SQLWriter... Do you mean (1) at least one single bond or (2) every bond single?' — no query is ever validated or executed. Exemplars 1 and 2 stall after schema exploration without a produced SQL.
issues.fix_direction: Change guidance so agents never emit a clarifying question as a terminal answer for BIRD-style tasks: adopt the most common/literal interpretation (here: molecules where all bonds are single, bond_type='-'), state the assumption in one line, and proceed to build and hand off SQL. Add a rule that SchemaLinker/SQLWriter must always produce an executable candidate query before any handoff to Validator.
